2606.29717.title: Optimizing Expert-Designed Crystal Graph Networks for Band-Gap Prediction with an Autonomous LLM Research Loop
2606.29717.paper_summary: Predicting a material's properties from its structure is a central, fast-advancing problem in computational materials science. A decade of work has produced standard public benchmarks and many published machine-learning models for the task (Dunn et al., 2020). The task's fixed metric and these baselines make it a natural setting for autonomous agent research (Karpathy, 2026). On the MatBench band-gap benchmark ($>$100k crystals), a general-purpose coding agent autonomously built the most accurate model trained without external pretraining, ahead of all seventeen expert-designed models reported for the task. A closer analysis shows it reached this by implementing known methods: either already standard in crystal neural-network models, or borrowed from other areas of machine learning. The contributing implementations include element-pair features on each message-passing edge and a crystal space-group embedding. The work not only demonstrates that LLM-agent autonomous research can optimize an expert-designed machine learning model for material property prediction, but also investigates the limitations of such autonomous research.
2606.29717.paper_summary_zh: 預測材料的性質從其結構中是一個在計算材料科學中核心且快速發展的問題。經過十年的努力，已經產生了標準的公共基準和許多發表的機器學習模型（Dunn et al., 2020）。這項任務的固定指標和這些基準使其成為自主代理研究的自然場景（Karpathy, 2026）。在MatBench帶隙基準（$>$100k晶體）上，一個通用編碼代理自主構建了最準確的模型，該模型在沒有外部預訓練的情況下進行訓練，超越了為該任務報告的所有十七個專家設計的模型。更深入的分析顯示，它是通過實施已知的方法達到這一點的：這些方法要麼在晶體神經網絡模型中已經標準化，要麼是借鑒自其他機器學習領域。貢獻的實現包括每個消息傳遞邊緣上的元素對特徵和晶體空間群嵌入。這項工作不僅展示了LLM代理自主研究能夠優化專家設計的機器學習模型以預測材料性質，還探討了這種自主研究的局限性。
2606.29717.author: Chenmu Zhang et.al.
